verses.text: Settled in cedar, David is troubled that the ark dwells in a tent, and proposes to build God a house; Nathan, sharing his zeal, says go ahead. A tender heart grieves to be better housed than the things of God — yet even good intentions must wait on God's word.

Settled in cedar, David is troubled that the ark dwells in a tent, and proposes to build God a house; Nathan, sharing his zeal, says go ahead. A tender heart grieves to be better housed than the things of God — yet even good intentions must wait on God's word.

That night God corrects them: in all His years with Israel He never asked for a house of cedar. God's ways are not bound by our ideas of what honours Him. He needs no building; He was content to dwell among His pilgrim people.

That night God corrects them: in all His years with Israel He never asked for a house of cedar. God's ways are not bound by our ideas of what honours Him. He needs no building; He was content to dwell among His pilgrim people.

God reminds David he was taken from the sheepfold and made great, then turns the plan around: 'the LORD will build thee an house.' David meant to build God a house; God will build David a dynasty. We can never outgive the God who delights to give.

God reminds David he was taken from the sheepfold and made great, then turns the plan around: 'the LORD will build thee an house.' David meant to build God a house; God will build David a dynasty. We can never outgive the God who delights to give.

God promises David's son will build the house, and 'I will stablish his throne for ever... I will be his father, and he shall be my son.' The covenant reaches past Solomon to Christ, the eternal Son of David, whose reign has no end.

David sits before the LORD, overwhelmed: 'Who am I, O LORD God, and what is mine house, that thou hast brought me hitherto?' The greater the grace, the smaller he feels. Wonder, not pride, is the right response to undeserved promises.

David praises God's uniqueness — 'there is none like thee' — and marvels at Israel, the people God redeemed to be His own for ever. Personal mercy widens into wonder at belonging to God's people. To be the redeemed of the LORD is the highest privilege.

David prays God's own promise back to Him: 'let the thing... be established for ever, and do as thou hast said,' resting in faith — 'thou blessest, O LORD, and it shall be blessed for ever.' True prayer asks God to perform what He has promised, and rests in His word.
